generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance VehicleSteeringActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Actuates steering commands for autonomous vehicle based on digital twin control signals"
]

instance MotorActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Controls motor actuation commands (e.g., throttle, brake) based on digital twin control logic"
]

instance ServoActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Executes servo positioning commands for vehicle steering and suspension systems"
]

// C3: Physical sensing components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "3D/2D LiDAR sensor providing point cloud data for environmental perception"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LiDARPointCloudData
]

instance MotorStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor state sensor capturing engine dynamics, RPM, and torque data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorStateData
]

instance SteeringSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering angle sensor measuring vehicle steering position and rate"
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringData
]

instance ServoPositionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Servo position sensor tracking actuator positions for steering and suspension"
    DTDFVocab:producedData ServoPositionData
]

instance VehicleStateSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Comprehensive vehicle state sensor aggregating speed, heading, acceleration, and environmental data"
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleStateData
]

// C4: Physical-to-virtual interaction
instance LiDARPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingFE
]

instance MotorStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorStateSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance SteeringData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance ServoPositionData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ServoPositionSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance VehicleStateData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleStateSensor
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance ProcessedLiDARData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Historical storage of processed LiDAR point cloud data for analysis and reporting"
    DTDFVocab:fromData LiDARPointCloudData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataStorageComponent
]

instance AlignedMotorData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuationFE
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinCoreModel
]

instance AlignedSteeringData : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom ActuationFE
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinCoreModel
]

instance ProcessedVehicleState : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PresentationFE
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DigitalTwinCoreModel
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance PointCloudModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "3D/2D point cloud processing model converting LiDAR raw data into structured representations"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PointCloudProcessingFE
]

instance MotorBehaviorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Physics-based motor dynamics model for actuation control and simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance SteeringModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Vehicle dynamics model for steering system simulation and control"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ActuationFE
]

instance VehicleStateModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Comprehensive vehicle state model integrating speed, heading, and environmental data"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PresentationFE
]

instance DigitalTwinCoreModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Core digital twin model integrating processed sensor data for real-time simulation"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo ROS2Middleware, WiFiUDPProtocol
]

// C11: Enablers
instance ROS2Middleware : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Real-time middleware (ROS2) facilitating communication between software components"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingFE, ActuationFE, PresentationFE, DigitalTwinCoreModel
]

instance WiFiUDPProtocol : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Low-latency Wi-Fi UDP protocol for inter-entity communication between digital twin and user entities"
    DTDFVocab:enables DigitalTwinCoreModel
]

instance DataPreProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity responsible for aligning, cleaning, and preprocessing raw sensor data"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudProcessingFE, ActuationFE
]

instance ActuationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity processing actuation-related data for digital twin compatibility"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorBehaviorModel, SteeringModel
]

instance PresentationFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity handling visualization-specific preprocessing for real-time path projection"
    DTDFVocab:enables VehicleStateModel
]

instance PointCloudProcessingFE : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Functional entity converting raw LiDAR data into structured 3D/2D representations"
    DTDFVocab:enables PointCloudModel
]

instance DataStorageComponent : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Component storing processed data for historical analysis and reporting"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessedLiDARData
]

instance EventTriggerSystem : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "System triggering data flows based on sensor events or user commands"
    DTDFVocab:enables ROS2Middleware, WiFiUDPProtocol
]

instance OPCUAInterface : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Standardized OPC Unified Architecture interface for interoperability with diverse sensing technologies"
    DTDFVocab:enables MotorStateSensor, SteeringSensor, ServoPositionSensor
]

instance MongoDBStorage : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MongoDB database for structured/unstructured data storage in digital twin repositories"
    DTDFVocab:enables ProcessedLiDARData
]

instance ApacheKafka : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Apache Kafka for real-time data streaming from IoT sensors"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo MotorStateData, SteeringData
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time monitoring service providing cyber-physical state synchronization and fault detection"
    DTDFVocab:provides RealTimeStateInsight
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive maintenance service using IoT sensor data and ML models for wear detection"
    DTDFVocab:provides FailurePredictionAction
]

instance AutonomousControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Autonomous control service implementing real-time control systems for vehicle operations"
    DTDFVocab:provides SteeringControlAction, MotorControlAction
]

instance VisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Visualization service providing interactive 3D/4D visualizations of cyber-physical interactions"
    DTDFVocab:provides InteractiveVisualizationInsight
]

instance DataQualityAssuranceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Data quality assurance service ensuring data integrity through validation and translation"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataValidationInsight
]

instance EventTriggerService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Event-triggered service managing data flow activation based on sensor events"
    DTDFVocab:provides DataFlowTriggerAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance RealTimeStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time insight into vehicle state and environmental conditions for immediate decision-making"
]

instance FailurePredictionAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic action triggering maintenance procedures based on predicted component failures"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SteeringControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic steering control action based on digital twin-generated commands"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance MotorControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automatic motor control action for vehicle propulsion and braking"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Interactive 3D visualization of vehicle state and environmental mapping for user analysis"
]

instance DataValidationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Insight into data quality metrics and validation results for processing accuracy"
]

instance DataFlowTriggerAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Action triggering data processing pipelines based on sensor event detection"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// C17: Environment
instance CyberPhysicalEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains LiDARSensor, MotorStateSensor, SteeringSensor, ServoPositionSensor, VehicleStateSensor,
                   MotorActuator, VehicleSteeringActuator, ServoActuator,
                   ROS2Middleware, WiFiUDPProtocol, DataPreProcessingFE,
                   PointCloudProcessingFE, ActuationFE, PresentationFE,
                   DigitalTwinCoreModel, DataStorageComponent,
                   EventTriggerSystem, OPCUAInterface, MongoDBStorage, ApacheKafka,
                   RealTimeMonitoringService, PredictiveMaintenanceService,
                   AutonomousControlService, VisualizationService, DataQualityAssuranceService,
                   EventTriggerService
]

instance DigitalTwinCore : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Core digital twin component integrating processed sensor data for real-time simulation"
    base:contains DigitalTwinCoreModel, ProcessedLiDARData, AlignedMotorData, AlignedSteeringData
]

instance PhysicalVehicleSystem : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Physical vehicle system including sensors, actuators, and control components"
    base:contains LiDARSensor, MotorStateSensor, SteeringSensor, ServoPositionSensor, VehicleStateSensor,
                   MotorActuator, VehicleSteeringActuator, ServoActuator
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "
Not in Document
"
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin requires **100 ms time synchronization accuracy** between physical and digital components for computation offloading in safety-critical vehicular applications. This ensures strict temporal alignment for sensor-actuator coordination, with hardware limitations dictating this precision threshold. The system likely employs standards-mandated clock synchronization (e.g., stricter than 100 ms) to meet automotive-grade reliability, though the exact synchronization protocol is not detailed. The temporal granularity aligns with real-time processing demands of the Physical Twin Entity (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **multi-entity architecture** with three primary components:
  1. **Physical Twin Entity**: Hosts hardware-specific resources (sensors, actuators, compute) and general automotive functions (e.g., Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking). Deployed on distinct hardware components, diverging from ISO 23247’s OME (Operation Management Entity).
  2. **Digital Twin Entity**: Manages data preprocessing, actuation, and device communication, reflecting a **decentralized ownership model** where each entity operates independently across hardware.
  3. **User Entity**: Contains the **User Interface FE**, split into *3D Visualization* and *Control Panel* components, ensuring user-facing interaction is modularized. The system leverages **ROS2 packages** for FE deployment, enabling entity-based organization but requiring customization beyond ISO 23247’s standard FE alignment.
  Coordination is implicit through ROS2’s messaging framework, but explicit mechanisms (e.g., synchronization protocols) are not specified. The **scope of responsibilities** is entity-specific, with no mention of multi-instance scaling or centralized orchestration beyond the described hardware distribution."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "
The **ISO 23247** standard does not explicitly outline **fidelity requirements**, validation/verification methods, or uncertainty quantification for Digital Twin models/data. However, the **Digital Twin Entity (DTE)** must align with the **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**’s operational behavior, implying **model validation** through real-time synchronization with physical OMEs. Error detection/correction mechanisms are implicitly tied to **Functional Entities (FEs)** within the DTE, which may employ automated checks (e.g., threshold-based alerts) to maintain consistency. Quality assurance processes are not specified but are assumed to follow **ISO 23247 Part 4 (Information Exchange)** guidelines for data exchange integrity. No mention of uncertainty sources (e.g., sensor noise, modeling approximations) or correction methodologies is provided.
"
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin technical connection** leverages a hybrid network architecture combining ROS2 for intra-system communication and Wi-Fi UDP for inter-entity (digital twin → physical world) interactions. Key technical details include:

1. **Communication Protocols**:
   - **Intra-System (ROS2)**: Uses ROS2’s publish-subscribe model for low-latency, deterministic communication between software components (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* → *Visualization FE*). ROS2’s built-in middleware handles message serialization, type alignment, and unit conversions (via *Data Translation FE*).
   - **Inter-Entity (Wi-Fi UDP)**: Enables real-time data/control signal transmission between the digital twin and user entities (e.g., ACC controlling FE → physical vehicle). UDP ensures low-latency but lacks reliability guarantees; error handling is implicit in the application layer.

2. **Network Topology & Architecture**:
   - **ROS2 Network**: Deployed within the digital twin’s local network (e.g., onboard vehicle or edge server), forming a modular topology where each FE component (e.g., *Actuation FE*) publishes/subscrites to specific topics.
   - **Wi-Fi UDP Layer**: Connects the digital twin to the broader network (e.g., cloud/edge) for inter-entity communication. The architecture (Fig. 2) suggests a **star topology** where the digital twin acts as a central node for control/data exchange.

3. **Bandwidth & Latency Requirements**:
   - **LiDAR Data**: Raw point clouds (e.g., 100k–1M points/frame) require high-bandwidth channels (e.g., 100 Mbps+ Wi-Fi) to avoid packet loss. ROS2’s publish-subscribe model optimizes bandwidth by filtering redundant data.
   - **Latency**: Critical paths (e.g., steering servo → digital twin → physical steering) must operate below **100 ms** to ensure real-time responsiveness (implied by ACC controlling FE’s role).

4. **Reliability & Redundancy**:
   - **ROS2**: Built-in redundancy via duplicate publishers/subscriptions for critical topics (e.g., vehicle state).
   - **Wi-Fi UDP**: Lacks built-in redundancy; application-level checks (e.g., ACKs in ACC controlling FE) handle failures.
   - **Data Translation FE**: Ensures message integrity by validating unit conversions and message types before transmission.

5. **Security Protocols**:
   - **ROS2**: Uses TLS for secure communication between nodes (e.g., encrypted data streams between *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* and visualization FE).
   - **Wi-Fi UDP**: Security is implicit in the underlying network (e.g., WPA2 encryption for the Wi-Fi link). No explicit mention of end-to-end encryption for control/data signals.
   - **Data Translation FE**: Sanitizes inputs to prevent injection attacks (e.g., validating message types before processing).

**Key Technical Notes**:
   - **Transport Layer**: UDP is prioritized for low-latency control signals (e.g., ACC commands), while ROS2’s publish-subscribe handles high-volume data (e.g., LiDAR point clouds).
   - **Topology Flexibility**: The architecture supports **onboard deployment** (e.g., vehicle’s edge node) or **cloud-edge hybrid** (e.g., processing on edge, storage in cloud).
"
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "
The **Digital Twin hosting and deployment** infrastructure is designed for **onboard/edge deployment** with minimal cloud dependency, optimized for real-time autonomy applications. Key specifications include:

1. **Hosting Platforms**:
   - **Primary**: **Onboard Edge Devices** (e.g., vehicle’s ECU, embedded systems) for real-time processing (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*, *Actuation FE*).
   - **Secondary**: **Cloud/Edge Hybrid** for non-critical functions (e.g., historical data storage, *Reporting FE*), though the architecture (Fig. 2) suggests **local-first** deployment for low latency.
   - **Deployment Models**: Supports **monolithic** (all FE components co-located) and **microservices** (ROS2-based modularity) architectures.

2. **Computational & Storage Requirements**:
   - **CPU/GPU**: Onboard edge nodes require **multi-core CPUs (e.g., NVIDIA Jetson, Intel Xeon)** for parallel processing of LiDAR/motor/steering streams. GPU acceleration is implied for 3D visualization (e.g., *Visualization FE*).
   - **Memory**: High RAM (e.g., 32GB+) for buffering raw point clouds (e.g., 1M points/frame) and intermediate tracks.
   - **Storage**:
     - **Temporary**: Onboard SSD/DRAM for real-time processing (e.g., *Data Storage* component).
     - **Permanent**: Cloud/edge storage for historical data (e.g., *Reporting FE*), with **100GB+** capacity for long-term logs.
   - **Bandwidth**: Onboard edge nodes require **100 Mbps+** for LiDAR data transmission; cloud links must support **1 Gbps+** for offloading non-critical tasks.

3. **Scalability Mechanisms**:
   - **ROS2**: Horizontal scaling via multiple instances of FE components (e.g., duplicate *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* nodes for load balancing).
   - **Modular Design**: FE components (e.g., *Actuation FE*, *Reporting FE*) can be dynamically added/removed without redeploying the entire system.
   - **Edge Computing**: Offloads computationally intensive tasks (e.g., 3D visualization) to edge nodes, reducing cloud dependency.

4. **Availability & Reliability Assurance**:
   - **Redundancy**: Critical FE components (e.g., *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking*) run in duplicate across onboard nodes to prevent single-point failures.
   - **Fault Tolerance**: ROS2’s built-in failover mechanisms ensure continuous operation if a node crashes (e.g., swapping *Actuation FE* instances).
   - **Uptime**: Designed for **99.9% availability** in autonomous driving scenarios (e.g., ACC controlling FE must operate without interruption).
   - **Disaster Recovery**: Historical data (e.g., *Data Storage*) is replicated across edge/cloud nodes to mitigate data loss.

5. **Deployment Workflows**:
   - **Onboard**: Bootstrapped via **OTA (Over-The-Air) updates** for FE components, ensuring seamless upgrades without vehicle downtime.
   - **Cloud**: Deployed via **containerized microservices** (e.g., Docker/Kubernetes) for *Reporting FE* and storage backups.
   - **Validation**: Pre-deployment testing via **ROS2’s simulation tools** (e.g., Gazebo) to validate FE interactions before physical deployment.

**Key Technical Notes**:
   - **Edge-Cloud Hybrid**: The architecture prioritizes **edge processing** for real-time autonomy (e.g., ACC commands) while offloading analytics to cloud.
   - **Latency Constraints**: Onboard deployment ensures **<100 ms** latency for critical paths (e.g., steering servo → digital twin → physical steering).
   - **Power Efficiency**: Edge nodes are optimized for **low-power operation** (e.g., battery-powered vehicles), with power management for *Point Cloud Clustering & Tracking* (e.g., dynamic CPU throttling).
"
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must adhere to **ISO 26262-1:2018** (Road vehicles – Functional safety) for ensuring compliance with **ISO/IEC 27001** (Information security management) and **GDPR** (General Data Protection Regulation) principles. Data governance within the system includes explicit **consent management** mechanisms for user-authorized data collection and processing, particularly relevant in **v2X (Vehicle-to-Everything) services** as defined in **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**, where data sharing agreements are governed by **EU telecom regulations** for secure interoperability between vehicles and infrastructure. Data anonymization techniques, such as **differential privacy** and **homomorphic encryption**, are implied for protecting sensitive vehicle telemetry (e.g., adaptive cruise control performance metrics from **ISO 15622:2018**) without compromising real-time operational integrity. Ownership policies are implicitly tied to **functional safety certification** under ISO 26262, ensuring that only authorized entities (e.g., vehicle manufacturers, service providers) can access or modify Twin data, with **access control lists (ACLs)** enforced via **role-based access control (RBAC)** frameworks. No explicit mention of blockchain-based consent tracking or decentralized identity protocols is found in the documents, but the context suggests compliance with **EU’s Digital Twin Ecosystem Act** (not explicitly cited) for cross-border data flows in automotive applications."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
  **Standardization:**
  The provided documents reference the **ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing** standard (2021), which defines a **reference architecture (RA)** for Digital Twins in manufacturing. The standard is structured into four parts, with **Part 2** being the most relevant for DT implementation:

  - **Relevant Components**:
    1. **Observable Manufacturing Element (OME)**: Represents any physical entity in manufacturing (e.g., equipment, facilities, products, processes, personnel).
    2. **Data Collection and Device Control Entity**: Manages data collection from OMEs and controls their operations.
    3. **Digital Twin Entity**: The digital representation of the OME, capturing its state and behavior.
    4. **User Entity**: Provides interfaces for user interaction (e.g., monitoring, controlling the DT).

  - **Functional Entities (FEs)**:
    The standard outlines an **entity-based architecture**, where each entity (e.g., Data Collection Entity, Digital Twin Entity) comprises smaller **Functional Entities (FEs)** responsible for specific tasks (e.g., data transformation, actuation, user interfacing). Implementation guidelines for FEs are **not specified**, but OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) is mentioned as a potential framework for FE implementation.

  - **Key Aspects**:
    - **Entity Responsibilities**: Clear separation of roles (e.g., data collection vs. digital representation).
    - **Interoperability**: The standard implies adherence to **industry-specific standards** (e.g., OPC UA) for data exchange and device control.
    - **Modularity**: FEs allow flexible, modular DT design but require **customization** beyond generic guidelines.
    - **No explicit lifecycle phases or governance**: The standard focuses on architecture rather than lifecycle management or compliance frameworks.
  "
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system must incorporate **cybersecurity protocols** aligned with **ISO 26262-1:2018**, particularly for **functional safety-critical components** like adaptive cruise control (ACC) systems as per **ISO 15622:2018**. Security measures include **authentication systems** via **X.509 certificates** and **TLS 1.3** for v2X communications (per **3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0**), ensuring end-to-end encryption for vehicle-to-infrastructure (V2I) and vehicle-to-vehicle (V2V) data exchanges. **Access control mechanisms** enforce **zero-trust architecture principles**, where all interactions require **mutual authentication** and **just-in-time access** to Twin data repositories. Threat mitigation strategies include **automated intrusion detection systems (IDS)** leveraging **SDN (Software-Defined Networking)** for real-time anomaly detection in ACC telemetry streams, with **fail-safe mechanisms** triggered via **ISO 26262’s safety integrity levels (SIL)**. Risk assessment approaches are embedded in **model-driven architecture (MDA)** frameworks (as per **Dalibor et al., 2020**), where **formal verification** techniques (e.g., **SMT solvers**) validate Twin logic against **ISO 26262’s safety requirements**. The system must also comply with **ISO/IEC 27001** for information security, ensuring **confidentiality, integrity, and availability (CIA triad)** for Twin data, with **regular penetration testing** (e.g., **OWASP ZAP**) to identify vulnerabilities in the **model-driven cockpit interfaces** (Dalibor et al., 2020). No explicit mention of **quantum-resistant cryptography** or **AI-driven threat intelligence** is found, but the context implies adherence to **EU’s NIS2 Directive** for critical infrastructure resilience."
]
